title: The Armisael Problem
wikitext:
[]: The Armisael Problem             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 19, 2008 7:28 am Since I wanted to approach this topic again... Replying to this . AsukaChan wrote: It felt like each Angel was a test in design and in intelligence. When one failed, the next tested something different. And this goes on until it reached Armisael, whose design (and behaviour) reminded me of biology. I mean, beyond the obvious double helix.. The fact that after fusing with Zero the big pillar of dead Angels appeared reminded me of how everything that exists now still carries genetic remnants of what came before. Like, how some horses are born with little vestigial toes because their genes still carry the remnants of its ancestor.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Mar 19, 2008 9:27 am I think its because Eva-00 is a clone of Adam. And I attribute the two-faced Sachiel to "LOL continuityerror"

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Mar 19, 2008 9:44 am I remember that junk about Shamshel's "genetic pattern in terms of spacing being a 99.8 percent match with human genes", whatever that meant. For EPIC FW, let us assume that gamma radiation from the N2 mine damaged Sachiel's genetics/wave pattern/whatever the hell so that Sachi decided to repair the damaged pattern by inserting the genes for the Second Face, and telepathically or however else altering the gene pattern in whatever storage Armisael borrowed it from. And V, none of the Evas really come across as "clones" of Adam or Lilith in the proper sense- the differences between the different Evas being a case in point. Apart from the nonsense above...*sigh*. No idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Mar 19, 2008 11:04 am A similar thing happens in the same episode (DC) where cute little Reis pop up underneath Shinji's skin, same thing happens again with the MPEs. In the case of Shinji this has something to do with the bio-contamination stuff and Rei/Lilith reaching out for him - when synchronising with the Evas if the plug depth is increased too far bio-contamination starts, and beyond that lies getting absorbed into the Eva like Shinji was in ep.#19 - Shinji is spat out of the core in a puddle of LCL after being absorbed, so maybe a similar dynamic is happening here with Eva-00 and the Angel. The next possiblity is that because Armisael isn't composed of Adamic material and is just a S^2 engine by itself so that when it became part of Eva-00, which is made of Adamic material, Armisael attempted to shape the Adamic material into it's own shape, but as Armisael has no self-image it formed instead the image that is shaped by others which for Armisael is all the other Angels (something similar is said in #25/#26 as to why all the Lilin have the same shape). Either way I don't think it has to do with genetic memory but more to do with this "yourself in the minds of others" and "others in your own mind" business.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 19, 2008 11:16 am Zuggy wrote: same thing happens again with the MPEs.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Mar 19, 2008 11:34 am Reichu wrote: There's no physical touching that time, though. (We don't see if the little Reis appear on Eva-01's hands, either.) If that has any relevance whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 19, 2008 12:01 pm Zuggy wrote: Doesn't Armisael flip between pattern blue and orange? What other Angels do that?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Mar 19, 2008 1:56 pm The theories that I think hold up are: 1-Eva-00 is a clone of Adam ("Clonal regeneration') and thus Armisael's fusion with her unlocked all of this. 2-Armisael, on merging with Eva-00 which is itself synched with Rei, gains Rei's perceptions of what other Angels "look like" and alters itself accordingly. Armisael's powers are inherently "shapeshifting and fusion" so I think we can give it some leeway in that regard. Armisael kind of reminds me of the "Coral Scub" (or whatever) from Eureka 7: it's not actually malevolent but literally the only method of communication it has is to physically fuse with something else. I do not believe that Armisael contains the "DNA" needed to make the other Angels. But the fusion of Eva-00 and Rei with an Angel is very chancy and by all means it was almost an (alternate version) of Third Impact: the body of Adam (cloned) uniting with its offspring's body and an Angel's soul merging with Lilith's. I've always wondered what would have happened had they not "pulled the plug" at the last minute.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Mar 19, 2008 4:44 pm I remember reading the theory that Armisael might have gathered the souls of the previous Angels and is attempting to "birth" them through Unit 00 to ease it's loneliness. How viable is this? (Though I'm not sure it really matters. As far as I'm aware the Angel Tower thing didn't even make it into the episode, proper.)

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Thu Mar 20, 2008 6:05 am AuraTwilight wrote: I remember reading the theory that Armisael might have gathered the souls of the previous Angels <snip>

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Mar 20, 2008 8:41 am Zuggy wrote: Reichu wrote: There's no physical touching that time, though. (We don't see if the little Reis appear on Eva-01's hands, either.) If that has any relevance whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 20, 2008 9:56 am AuraTwilight wrote: I remember reading the theory that Armisael might have gathered the souls of the previous Angels and is attempting to "birth" them through Unit 00 to ease it's loneliness. How viable is this?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Mar 20, 2008 12:32 pm Reichu wrote: I've never seen any substance to the concept of "genetic memory" in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Mar 20, 2008 12:42 pm At any rate I don't see how the soul theory could really hold up, or why Armisael would be "collecting" them particularly.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 20, 2008 1:06 pm V wrote: Ontogeny recapitulates phylogeny (well actually, that was revealed to be crackpot science but....)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Mar 20, 2008 1:53 pm Reichu wrote: Why do you say that? (I've never seen any substance to the concept of "genetic memory" in NGE.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 20, 2008 2:22 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: It was just me saying it's a possibility. More likely is the concept of... soul memory ?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Mar 20, 2008 3:17 pm Things I've considered (which are probably all massively fanwankerific, but no matter): The entry plug is called "the throne of a soul". It acts as a mock-womb, and it is inserted through an artificial port in the Eva's upper back, one of NGE's many mock-vaginae. Are the souls being injected into the entry plug and "reborn" out of the port to form new bodies from their surrogate mother's flesh? But why would they remain attached and unfinished...?

[]: Re: The Armisael Problem             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Fri Mar 21, 2008 4:44 am Reichu wrote: If the Angels' wacky forms are part of their genetic makeup, then how can such a minute difference in DNA between them and us possibly be accounted for? (It's enough of a stretch as is.) There are other things, too, but that's one place to start.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Mar 21, 2008 12:50 pm What exactly is the criteria by which they can tell that the the Angels are human? (I'm not disputing that this isn't true, I just need to know what's being analyzed that comes back with the answer of 'human'.) Even among humans, there's a ridiculous amount of genetic variation. Large chunks of the DNA strand might be cut out of one area and pasted into another (when comparing two individuals). Or the genes might be switched off for whatever reason. It's like how a lot of plants have the same kind and amount of DNA as a human, but most of that doesn't seem to be encoding anything. It's just THERE, for no particular reason (that we know of as this time). It might just be there to help -- in whatever way -- the structure of the rest of the organism.
